questions.question: What is Writing Files and why is it important in Python?
questions.answer: Writing Files is a core Python feature that enables structured, clean programming.

questions.question: What are common performance pitfalls when using Writing Files?
questions.answer: Avoid unnecessary memory allocations or deep loop nesting.

questions.question: How is Writing Files implemented internally in CPython?
questions.answer: CPython manages it through bytecode instructions and evaluation stack frames.
